name: Core Rules
purpose: Framework-free Domino Poker domain logic: tile model, shuffle, players, legal play, bidding, scoring, round/game state, standings, and AI heuristics.
risks: Rule changes affect both single-player and multiplayer.
The human-style shuffle is intentional and shared; do not replace it casually.
0-6 ace/trump context, legal-follow rules, scoring, and tiebreakers are high-risk.
Core must stay independent of React, Node server, DB, WebSocket, and shared protocol infrastructure.

name: Core Multiplayer
purpose: Deterministic multiplayer state-machine layer around core rules: setup, commands, events, turn deadlines, timeout auto-actions, inactivity, snapshots, replay, legal helpers, and invariants.
risks: Must remain seed-driven and deterministic; no Math.random or Date.now in decisions.
State changes must go through applyCommand.
Snapshots must remain privacy-safe; public snapshots must not leak opponent hands.
Lobby/membership transport concerns belong in apps/server, not this package.

name: Shared Protocol
purpose: Public protocol and DTO package for server/client communication, room DTOs, protocol versioning, errors, avatar catalog, account title helpers, leaderboard DTOs, and the authoritative `GAME_LANGUAGES` runtime/type list.
risks: clientMessages.ts is an external input boundary; keep Zod validation and max lengths strict.
serverEvents.ts imports core MP event/snapshot types, so shared is protocol-facing but not fully domain-neutral.
Changing room/event/message contracts requires server, web, load-test, and shared tests.
Keep `GAME_LANGUAGES` aligned with `apps/web/lib/i18n.ts`; server account-language validation uses this enum, while DB migration 0013 deliberately removed the old en/lv CHECK.
serverEvents.ts also carries fanout/event-bus contracts used by ServerEventBus/PostgresEventBus.

name: Web App
purpose: Next.js browser app for single-player, multiplayer lobby/table, MP lobby chat emoji and translation UI, optional account UI, 21-locale localization, audio, responsive desktop/mobile layouts, UI primitives, PWA registration/install prompt, version display, and token-driven styling.
risks: Multiplayer UI must render server snapshots and send intents only; server remains authoritative.
AppShell is a central shell and can become a god component if unrelated workflow state is added.
DominoPokerGame owns substantial single-player application workflow around core state.
CSS entry globals.css is import-only; feature CSS belongs under apps/web/styles.
PWA/service-worker cache can hide asset/UI changes during manual checks.

UI text should flow through localization files and passed labels. Flat UI strings live in `lib/locales/*.ts` and must satisfy `AppStrings`; MP rules docs for the 19 added locales live in `lib/locales/mpRules/*` and are selected from `mpRulesContent.ts`.

The 21 UI locales must stay aligned across `appStrings`, the visible `locales` selector, `packages/shared/src/leaderboard.ts` `GAME_LANGUAGES`, and `mpRulesContent`. System emails intentionally stay LV/EN through `emailLocale()`.
MP chat translation targets `labels.localeCode`, so new locales normally do not require changing the translation API contract.
gameTableView.ts may derive playable-tile hints from core, but multiplayer acceptance/rejection remains server-authoritative.
Theme/PWA/version changes cross `VERSION`, `next.config.ts`, `tokens.css`, `sw.js`, and deploy behavior.
Desktop/mobile multiplayer table split requires checking both MpDesktopTable and MpMobileTable before layout changes.

name: Multiplayer Server
purpose: Authoritative Node multiplayer application layer: HTTP/WS composition, gateway/hub/fanout, routing, room lifecycle, timers, reconnect/session identity, chat, optional chat translation, auth integration, persistence hooks, metrics, and cleanup.
risks: RoomEngine is the per-room single-writer state mutation path.
messageRouter.ts is large and mixes routing with lifecycle/finalization concerns; avoid adding rule logic there.
Server time is authoritative; do not trust client timestamps for correctness.
PostgreSQL multi-instance foundations exist, but active room failover is not complete.
Resource lifecycle invariants around game-over teardown, sessions, room TTL, and rate limits are high-risk.
Gateway changes can affect heartbeat, backpressure, superseded sockets, reconnect, and cross-instance fanout.
Session/identity changes can break reconnect tokens, display ids, durable session cleanup, and public seat profile mapping.
Server internal flow should remain gateway -> router -> room manager/engine -> core, with storage/auth/session as infrastructure boundaries.
Chat translation is an optional HTTP side route; keep Google credentials server-only, validate bounded text/language input, cache repeated translations, and preserve rate/character limits.

name: Server Auth
purpose: Optional account/profile layer: registration, login, bearer tokens, password reset, profile/stats, public identity resolution, password hashing, and email sender abstraction.
risks: Auth is optional and must not become required for single-player or anonymous multiplayer.
Raw auth tokens must never be logged or stored; storage keeps hashes.
WEB_ORIGIN/TRUST_PROXY/CORS/rate-limit behavior is security-sensitive.
Password reset tokens must stay single-use, hashed, and URL-hash based on the client.

name: Server Storage
purpose: Persistence boundary and adapters for matches, events, stats, chat, auth, avatars, durable sessions, room leases, migrations, and database health.
risks: schema.ts is the single DDL source for both SQLite and PostgreSQL (latest is 0013_user_preferences_language_open; then 0012_chat_blocked_words, 0011_bans, 0010_coin_ledger_open_reason). Most migrations are CREATE ... IF NOT EXISTS, but 0010 AND 0013 are dialect-branched: PG drops a constraint via a pg_constraint DO block, SQLite rebuilds the table inside BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT. 0013 relaxes the user_preferences.language CHECK (2 langs -> 21) so language is validated only by the Zod GAME_LANGUAGES enum (like coin_ledger.reason).
Migration IDs are production identity; append new migrations only at the end.

SQLite migrate() runs each migration `up` via db.exec WITHOUT a surrounding transaction (node:sqlite exec auto-commits per statement); a multi-step migration that must be atomic (e.g. a table rebuild) MUST wrap itself in BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT. PG sends each `up` as one multi-statement simple-query = one implicit transaction (already atomic).
StoragePort behavior should be covered by the shared storage contract tests.
SQLite files under data/ are runtime data and ignored; do not commit them.
PostgreSQL pool settings affect storage and event bus pools.
Storage implementations also provide contracts used by auth and durable session code, not only match/event persistence.
Room leases are PostgreSQL multi-instance coordination state and must stay aligned with RoomOwnershipGuard.

CoinStore (gold wallet) is implemented by both SQLite and PostgreSQL alongside StoragePort/AuthStore; applyLedger must stay atomic + idempotent in both backends and is covered by the shared storage contract. See the Coin Economy (Gold) module.

name: Web Auth Client
purpose: Browser-side account integration: HTTP API calls, auth token state, profile refresh, persisted account language, avatar URL resolution/upload preparation, and localized title labels.
risks: Auth tokens live in browser storage; use safeStorage and never put tokens into URLs.
Avatar upload is intentionally client-side resized/compressed before POST.
Reset password token is read from URL hash and immediately removed from location.
Account language uses shared `GameLanguage`; password reset and contact emails accept only LV/EN, so callers must narrow via `emailLocale()` before hitting email routes.

name: Multiplayer Simulators
purpose: Headless deterministic multiplayer game simulation through core multiplayer applyCommand, with random legal bids/moves, timeout/disconnect scenarios, and invariant checks.
risks: Decision randomness must be seeded and reproducible.
Simulator should import MP via the public @domino-poker/core/multiplayer entrypoint.
Do not duplicate game rules inside the simulator.

name: Multiplayer Load Test
purpose: Local WebSocket load generator that speaks the real shared protocol and records client/server stability, latency, and resource metrics.
risks: Requires a running server and should use disposable/local data when generating chat/game traffic.
High latency is saturation signal; dropped sockets/server death are stability failures.
This is a tool, not production server logic.

name: SP Bot Benchmark
purpose: Headless single-threaded benchmark of the trained ISMCTS bot's CLIENT-side cost: plays full self-play games with the exact calls the browser Web Worker makes (chooseInclusionBid, IsmctsSearcher iterated in MOVE_CHUNK=64 blocks) and reports per-decision ms per difficulty (Medium/Hard/Epic). The bot runs client-side in single-player, so this characterizes device cost, NOT server scaling.
risks: Mirror botWorker.ts exactly (MOVE_CHUNK=64, inclusion reward, mulberry32) or the timings stop reflecting the real bot.
Node desktop/VPS numbers are an optimistic baseline; a low-end phone is ~3-6x slower (the high end). They do not represent server load (single-player is client-side).
Its deal/shuffle is benchmark-internal feed only — NOT the game's shuffle (packages/core), which stays untouched.

name: E2E Tests
purpose: Playwright browser coverage for single-player flow, multiplayer smoke, layout regression, dialog accessibility, and storage resilience.
risks: Playwright config starts apps/server from dist, so server must be built before test:web.
Browser selectors must distinguish single-player Play from multiplayer Lobby/Start actions.
Tests are serial with one Chromium worker.

name: Tooling And CI
purpose: Root build/test/lint/typecheck scripts, Node/runtime pins, TypeScript/ESLint/Playwright config, and GitHub Actions CI.
risks: Node version pins are tied to node:sqlite support.
Shared/server tests may require core/shared/server dist builds before runtime execution.
Run typecheck, lint, test, test:web, and build sequentially rather than racing generated outputs.
Node 24 is pinned for local/CI/deploy and package engines require Node >=24.0.0 with engine-strict (geoip-lite needs >=24; node:sqlite needs >=22.5).
deploy.sh is ignored/local but mutates VERSION and sw.js, commits, and pushes to main.

name: Deployment And Operations
purpose: Tracked deployment examples, reverse proxy examples, backup guidance, and environment template.
risks: Do not commit real secrets, DB URLs, host/IP values, or local deploy scripts.
Reverse proxy config must preserve WebSocket upgrade for /ws and protect /metrics.
For production behind proxy, SERVER_HOST should usually be 127.0.0.1 and TRUST_PROXY only when proxy is trusted.
Docker image builds/runs only the server dependency chain, not the Next web client.
deploy.sh builds server and web on the VPS and restarts both domino-poker and domino-web, but only domino-poker.service is tracked under deploy/.
Reverse proxies must route /ws and /auth/* to the server port and should pair trusted X-Forwarded-For handling with TRUST_PROXY=true only behind a trusted proxy.

name: Documentation And Local Planning
purpose: Public README/rules docs, screenshots used by README, and local planning notes when present; only tracked docs should be assumed in a clean clone.
risks: Implementation and tests are authoritative when docs disagree.
Local ignored docs such as docs/DEPLOYMENT.md, docs/SCALING.md, docs/DB_MIGRATION.md, docs/TODO/*, and docs/mockups/* may not exist in clean clones.
Do not link ignored local docs from public README unless they become tracked.
Screenshots are binary README assets; do not treat them as source-of-truth architecture docs.
Ignored local docs under docs/ may exist in this working tree but should not be assumed in clean clones.

name: Local Runtime Data
purpose: Local SQLite runtime data directory for default server persistence.
risks: Only data/.gitkeep should be tracked.
data/*.sqlite, data/*.sqlite-wal, and data/*.sqlite-shm are ignored runtime files.
Use disposable DATABASE_URL values for tests/load runs that should not pollute local state.

name: Server Gateway And Sessions
purpose: Server connection/session boundary for WebSocket clients, active connection registry, in-process/PostgreSQL fanout, display ids, reconnect tokens, durable sessions, and delivery helpers.
risks: Protocol parsing, auth resolution, heartbeat, reconnect, supersede, and backpressure are all cross-cutting client/server behavior.
Durable sessions and display ids must not leak private tokens or break anonymous multiplayer.
PostgreSQL fanout is for multi-instance broadcast coordination; it is not active room-state failover.

name: AI Bot (trained ISMCTS)
purpose: Self-contained strong Domino Poker bot (hidden-hand, ISMCTS max^n). Three sub-packages: engine (pure rules/scoring/state/rng), ai (ISMCTS search, determinization, Monte Carlo bidding, inference, profiling), and bot-adapter (Node worker_threads AiClient). WIRED IN: engine + ai are now ROOT npm workspaces consumed by the browser single-player game; bot-adapter is NOT (Node-only, bypassed in the browser).
risks: engine + ai are root npm workspaces (added to root package.json workspaces); ai/package.json depends on engine via exact '1.0.0' (NOT pnpm 'workspace:*', which npm rejects). bot-adapter is intentionally NOT a workspace (Node worker_threads).

dist/ is gitignored AND deploy.sh tar-excludes 'dist' at any depth, so the VPS only receives src; apps/web prebuild/predev builds @domino-poker/engine then @domino-poker/ai (tsc -b) before the web build, and CI builds them before typecheck. Build engine before ai (ai/tsconfig references engine).

Do NOT keep a local pnpm node_modules under packages/ai_bot: its broken tsc bin shadows root typescript for the npm-workspace build. VPS never has it (tar excludes node_modules), so root tsc is used there.

Has its own engine duplicating Domino rules independently of packages/core; the browser bridge (botBridge.ts) maps core {side1,side2} tiles <-> bot bitmask indices and reconstructs PlayerView. Trump/ace ordering and bid=-1 sentinel are verified-identical between the two engines.
eslint ignores packages/ai_bot/** (vendored sub-project, fails this repo's no-unused-vars).
Decisions are seedable (mulberry32); no Math.random/Date.now. Not yet wired into multiplayer (server still uses core/aiService bots).

name: Web SP Bot Bridge
purpose: Browser-side glue that makes the trained ISMCTS bot drive all 3 single-player CPU seats, replacing the old core/aiService heuristic AI in SP. botBridge.ts converts core GameState <-> bot PlayerView (main thread, cheap) and delegates the heavy decideBid (Monte Carlo) / decideMove (ISMCTS) to a Web Worker (botWorker.ts) so the UI never blocks. difficulty.ts holds the lightweight Medium/Hard/Epic budgets (so the lobby can import it without pulling the heavy bot). liveness.ts guarantees the AI turn never stalls if the bot returns an illegal move.
risks: Only botWorker.ts imports @domino-poker/ai (heavy ISMCTS); Turbopack bundles it as a separate Web Worker asset, so it is OFF the main thread AND out of the main page bundle. botBridge.ts (main thread) imports only @domino-poker/engine helpers + the worker URL. DominoPokerGame is also code-split via next/dynamic(ssr:false) to keep the game UI out of the lobby bundle.

Keep DEFAULT_DIFFICULTY/BOT_DIFFICULTIES in difficulty.ts (no heavy imports) so AppShell/LobbyScreen don't drag the bot back into the lobby chunk. Difficulty (Medium 8000/1000, Hard 30000/3000, Epic 50000/5000) is chosen in the Settings dialog (lobby) and threaded AppShell -> DominoPokerGame -> botBridge; read via a ref so it doesn't churn the AI effect.

The AI turn effect is async with turnKey + cancelled guards; decideBid/decideMove are async (await the worker). The worker has a 20s per-request timeout backstop and onerror handling so a worker crash/hang rejects the call and the liveness net engages (it never permanently stalls the turn).

Old heuristic bots (core/aiService makeAIBid/selectAITile/selectNumber) are NO LONGER used in SP, but they are NOT dead: multiplayer still uses them via core/multiplayer autoBid/autoMove -> server GameDirector. Do not delete them.

name: Coin Economy (Gold)
purpose: Cross-cutting virtual gold-coin currency on top of the optional account system. Server-authoritative: per-account balance + append-only ledger in the DB, a wallet application layer, single-player win rewards gated by a server-issued one-time token, balance surfaced to the web, multiplayer paid rooms (host-set entry fee, prize pot split 70/30 to the top-2 registered humans) shipped server-side (Phase 3), the paid-room web UI (Phase 4: fee input gated to logged-in hosts, coin badges in room lists, table pot, live WALLET_UPDATED balance), and rules/i18n coverage across all 21 UI locales (flat strings via `AppStrings`, MP paid-room docs via `mpRulesContent` + `locales/mpRules/*`). Anonymous play has no wallet, cannot join paid rooms, and earns nothing.
risks: Money is server-authoritative: every balance change goes through WalletService.applyLedger (atomic + idempotent by (user_id, reason, ref)); never mutate coin_balances directly. SQLite uses a transaction; Postgres uses an ensure-row + FOR UPDATE transaction so concurrent same-user ops serialize.

packages/shared/src/economy.ts is the single source of amounts/splits (STARTING_COINS=5000, SP_REWARDS medium/hard/epic=50/100/300, POT_SPLIT 70/30, MIN_ENTRY_FEE/MAX_ENTRY_FEE, splitPot remainder→1st / single→100%). Do not hardcode these elsewhere; the server enforces, the web only displays.

Phase 3 MP money: entry fee/refund/payout reuse WalletService.applyLedger with NO new migration. Idempotency keys: mp_entry/mp_refund ref=entryId (per seat-occupation, NOT roomId, so refund→rejoin is a real new debit); mp_payout ref=matchId (one payout per user per match). LobbyManager owns pot bookkeeping (create/join +fee, leave −fee, forfeit/mid-game teardown unchanged so forfeited fees stay in the pot, D5). Seat carries opaque entry{entryId,payerUserId}.

Phase 3 orchestration lives in messageRouter (createPaidRoom/joinPaidRoom): debit-then-commit-seat with full rollback on any post-debit failure; isUserSeated is re-checked synchronously right before the seat commit to close the concurrent duplicate-userId race; leave/delete/TTL refunds go through tryRefund (idempotent) with an in-memory pendingRefunds retry queue drained by the periodic sweepExpiredRooms. MatchPayoutService settles the pot at GAME_OVER (top-2 registered non-forfeited humans; bots/forfeiters excluded; idempotent by matchId; fire-and-forget). Residual (R3): refund-retry + payout are in-memory, so a process crash can strand a refund/payout (no DB outbox; accepted for virtual currency / single-VPS). Fully abandoned paid games leave the pot unpaid.

SP reward anti-cheat (D3): reward difficulty comes from the server-issued one-time token (SpRewardTokens), not the client; /sp/reward also enforces auth + min duration + per-user rate limit + a HARD daily cap clamped under a per-user in-process lock. Tokens are in-memory/single-instance (matches the per-instance RateLimiter assumption). Residual: client still asserts placement.

Coin tables (migration 0007_coin_wallet: coin_balances, coin_ledger) follow the schema.ts rules — append-only, never renumber. coin_balances keeps the balance>=0 CHECK; coin_ledger keeps the delta<>0 CHECK and the UNIQUE(user_id, reason, ref) idempotency index, but migration 0010_coin_ledger_open_reason DROPPED the reason CHECK enum (D6) — reason is now the authoritative TS LedgerReason union (signup/sp_reward/mp_entry/mp_refund/mp_payout/admin_adjust), enforced by WalletService, not the DB. Add a new reason in the union; no migration needed.
Registration grants the signup bonus; WalletService.getBalance is repair-on-read so /auth/me and WELCOME also backfill existing accounts idempotently.
WELCOME carries optional goldBalance and /auth/me returns balance — both additive/optional, backward compatible with old clients.
Single-instance caveat: the SP daily-cap lock and token store are per-process; a true multi-instance SP mode would need the cap pushed into the DB transaction.

DB-level money correctness is validated against a real multi-instance PostgreSQL in apps/server/test/storage/coinConcurrency.integration.test.ts (run via `npm run test:postgres:docker`): concurrent distinct-ref debits never overdraft (FOR UPDATE serializes; exactly floor(balance/fee) succeed), same-key debit/refund/payout apply exactly once under concurrency (no double-apply, no unique-violation crash), and two separate PostgresStorage pools (two app instances) racing the same row keep all invariants. The orchestration-layer in-memory refund-retry/payout caches are the only non-DB-durable part (R3).

name: Player Statistics (Deep Stats)
purpose: Cross-cutting deep player statistics for registered users (bid accuracy + placement distributions; NOT a competitive leaderboard — they drive no rewards/ranking). One generalized player_game_results table (migration 0008, mode sp/mp discriminator, per-game rows, idempotent by id) via the PlayerStatsStore capability. Bid accuracy needs per-round bid-vs-tricks, which the engine resets each round, so the authoritative ROUND_RESULT event was enriched with playerResults captured pre-reset. SP is client-reported + server-validated via POST /sp/complete (token snapshots difficulty+roundCount; sum===roundCount check; coins for 1st/2nd; independent idempotency + consumed-token replay -> stable success). MP is server-authoritative via MpStatsRecorder (room-owner lifecycle, accumulates from ROUND_RESULT.playerResults before GAME_OVER, persists mp:{matchId}:{userId} rows; records all MP games incl. with bots). Read via GET /stats (lazy, own handler) -> PlayerStatsService.getStats composition; profile Statistics tab renders hand-rolled token-only CSS bars.
risks: player_game_results (migration 0008) is one generalized table with a mode sp/mp discriminator + per-game rows, idempotent by id (sp:{gameToken} / mp:{matchId}:{userId}). assertValidGameResult is a DB-agnostic guard (placement 1..4, non-negative counters, bid_met+bid_exceeded+bid_missed===round_count, sp=>difficulty / mp=>no difficulty); DDL CHECKs are the backstop. Aggregates computed on read via GROUP BY (verified identical on SQLite + Postgres via the storage contract / docker).

ROUND_RESULT was enriched with playerResults:{playerId,bid,tricksWon}[] captured PRE-reset in core applyStartNextRound. GAME_EVENT.event is zod-passthrough, so the field survives cross-instance fanout — do not add strict event schemas that would strip it.

SP stats are client-reported (the exact/over/under split and placement), validated only by sum===token.roundCount; acceptable because stats drive nothing competitive (coins still derive only from token difficulty + placement). POST /sp/complete: peek (do NOT consume) -> validate -> record -> credit (1st/2nd, >=5s) -> consume AFTER success; independent idempotency (row id + ledger key); consumed-token replay returns a stable 200 via getGameResultOwner (cross-user -> 409). Token TTL is 2h. Known future edge: credit fails AND token expires before retry can lose that reward (mitigated by TTL; full fix = reward_claimed flag).

MP stats: MpStatsRecorder is a room-owner-lifecycle sibling of OutcomeRecorder; it accumulates from ROUND_RESULT.playerResults in onMatchEvents (runs before onMatchFinished in the same RoomManager.onEventsAppended, so the final round counts) and persists at GAME_OVER. Owner-only + idempotent rows; an owner crash before GAME_OVER loses that match's stats (same class as OutcomeRecorder). Records ALL MP games incl. with bots (same-account multi-seat is impossible via messageRouter.isUserSeated).

/sp/reward is app-unused (the client calls /sp/complete) but kept + tested (deprecated); startSchema.rounds.default(7) keeps it functional. Deferred future hardening (non-blocking): reward_claimed robustness, full-game client accumulation test, e2e MP stats-row wiring test, populated live visual check.

name: Admin Panel
purpose: Separate Game Manager admin portal (PlayFab/Azure style, reduced scope), English-only. Phases 0-4 COMPLETE and D2 deployed at https://admin.domino-poker.com: see docs/TODO/admin-panel-plan.md + docs/TODO/admin-api.md. Phase 4A added read-only analytics (AdminAnalyticsService: overview/activity[.csv]/segments/leaderboard over existing tables, no new migration) and Phase 4B added governance (AdminPlayerGovernanceService: full allowlisted JSON export + D5 hard-delete = scrub matches.players_json + anonymize login_attempts PII + FK cascade, with a pre-delete snapshot saved to the audit log as the backup). Phase 3 added bans (BanService: migration 0011_bans, enforced at login + HTTP token revoke + WS handshake hard-reject + WS-upgrade IP-ban + live disconnect) and chat moderation (ChatModerationService: migration 0012_chat_blocked_words, in-memory blocked-word filter in LobbyChat + admin chat announcement). Earlier: Fully separate auth from player auth: single admin (owner), .env scrypt password + Resend email OTP 2FA, session in HttpOnly+Secure+SameSite=Strict cookie + CSRF double-submit. Server module apps/server/src/admin/ (AdminStore capability, AdminAuthService, AdminAuditService, AdminPlayerService read-only, AdminPlayerWriteService write ops, adminRoutes /admin/login|verify|logout|session|audit + Phase 1 player reads /admin/players[/:id[/logins]] + Phase 2 player writes (PATCH /admin/players/:id account, PATCH .../stats, POST .../coins, POST .../reset-password hard, POST .../send-reset-email soft) + requireAdmin guard, cookies); apps/admin is a Next.js app on port 3001 (login password->OTP, Audit History, Players search + profile + login history + management forms). Phase 2 also added migration 0010_coin_ledger_open_reason (D6: drops coin_ledger.reason CHECK; reason now enforced by the TS LedgerReason union) and AuthStore admin write methods (adminUpdateAccount/adminSetUserStats/adminInvalidateCredentials) + WalletService.adminAdjust. Phase 4A.2 (D4) added country/platform analytics segments: AdminStore.successfulLoginUserIps/successfulLoginUserAgents return DISTINCT (user,ip)/(user,user_agent) pairs over successful logins (no new migration; ip/user_agent already on login_attempts), and AdminAnalyticsService buckets DISTINCT users per country/platform (Set<userId>, so one user from two IPs in one country counts once). Country derived at read time via CountryResolver port (GeoipCountryResolver wraps geoip-lite, isolated behind the port + loaded via createRequire only when admin enabled); platform via classifyPlatform pure fn (mobile/desktop/other). Raw IP/UA never leave the service; geoTruncated flags partial counts at the distinct-pair cap.
risks: Admin routes are mounted ONLY when config.admin.enabled (ADMIN_PASSWORD_HASH set) AND an EmailSender exists AND storage isAdminStore; otherwise /admin/* 404s. requireAdmin is the authoritative guard (CORS/ADMIN_WEB_ORIGIN is not security by itself).

Password is scrypt (reuses auth/passwords.ts verifyPassword), NEVER plain/sha256. OTP codes + session tokens are stored only as sha256 hash. OTP is single-active-challenge (new code deletes prior), single-use, attempts-capped, TTL'd; consume is atomic (SQLite tx / PG FOR UPDATE).
Migration 0009_admin (admin_sessions, admin_login_codes, admin_audit_log, login_attempts) follows schema.ts append-only rules; login_attempts.user_id FK is ON DELETE SET NULL.
EVERY admin mutation must write an AdminAuditService.record row (Codex invariant); covered by adminRoutes.integration test.
CSRF double-submit: mutating routes require X-CSRF-Token header == admin_csrf cookie (csrf cookie is NOT HttpOnly so the SPA can read it). Session cookie Secure flag is off in dev (http), on in prod.
login_attempts is recorded via authRoutes.onLoginAttempt hook (fire-and-forget) wired in index.ts only when storage isAdminStore; the player AuthService stays transport-agnostic (no IP).
apps/admin is a root workspace + CI build and has a production deploy path through domino-admin systemd + the Caddy admin subdomain. ADMIN_* and Resend env still gate whether routes mount.

Phase 1 player reads: searchPlayers sorts by last successful login via a correlated MAX(login_attempts.created_at) subquery; ORDER BY MUST use `last_login_at DESC NULLS LAST` (a standalone aliased term) — the `<alias> IS NULL` expression form is invalid in PostgreSQL though SQLite tolerates it (dialect-parity trap). LIKE uses ESCAPE '\' + escapeLike over the already-lowercased username_norm/email_norm. Read routes are GET → requireAdmin without CSRF.

Phase 2 player writes (AdminPlayerWriteService): audit lives IN the service (every mutation records its own AdminAuditService row → 'no mutation without audit' is structural), NOT in routes; coins audit ONLY when the ledger actually changed (applied=true) so idempotent retries don't double-audit. Mutation + audit are two separate writes (not one cross-capability tx) — coverage, not atomicity (accepted for a single-admin panel). Coin adjust is idempotent by a client-or-server adjustmentId (ledger ref), minBalance 0 (no negative balance). Stats correction SETS the user_stats aggregate (games=wins+losses), per-game player_game_results untouched (D3). Hard password reset sends the email BEFORE invalidating the password/sessions (never lock a user out); editing email also deletes unused reset tokens (old-email recovery risk). All mutating routes require CSRF; CORS Allow-Methods includes PATCH.

Migration 0010_coin_ledger_open_reason (D6) drops the coin_ledger.reason CHECK enum so new reasons (admin_adjust, ...) need no per-reason migration; reason is now enforced ONLY by the TS CoinStore.LedgerReason union (WalletService). PG drops the constraint via a dynamic pg_constraint lookup (DO block matching the check def on `reason` — NOT the auto-generated name, and robust to PG rewriting IN→= ANY(ARRAY)). SQLite has no DROP CONSTRAINT so it rebuilds the table wrapped in BEGIN IMMEDIATE/COMMIT (migrate()'s exec is not auto-transactional); the rebuild keeps the delta<>0 CHECK + FK CASCADE + idempotency UNIQUE index. Verified on real PG (docker) + a targeted SQLite rebuild test (data/FK/index preservation + idempotent re-run).

Phase 3 bans (BanService, migration 0011_bans): a ban targets user_id AND/OR ip (table-level CHECK user_id IS NOT NULL OR ip IS NOT NULL); active = revoked_at IS NULL AND (kind='permanent' OR expires_at>now); temporary bans auto-expire (owner-approved expires_at). Enforcement (D1) is push-based, NO per-WS-message DB lookup: AuthService.banChecker rejects login after the password check before token issue AND re-checks after issue to revoke the just-minted token (closes the login↔ban race — Codex); authRoutes does an IP-ban 403 at login (+ post-login re-check); the WS gateway HARD-rejects a banned authed HELLO (not a silent anon downgrade) via an injected isUserBanned; wsTransport socket.destroys a banned IP at the HTTP upgrade (before the socket is accepted; fail-open on DB error); gateway.disconnectUser tears down live authed sessions (called by BanService.onUserBanned AFTER the ban persists). banUser persists FIRST then enforces (deleteUserAuthTokens + onUserBanned + best-effort sendBanNotice + audit). A banned account can still play ANONYMOUSLY unless its IP is also banned (D1(d), documented policy). KNOWN accepted residual: a microsecond WS HELLO↔ban race (ban persists between the handshake ban-check and identity attach) can let one authed WS session through — single-instance limitation, documented at WebSocketGateway.ts. revokeBan only affects an ACTIVE ban (expired temporary → not_active). index.ts breaks the BanService↔gateway cycle with a const gatewayHolder (late-bound .current). trustProxy is threaded consistently (clientIp ignores XFF when trustProxy=false). IP-ban input is validated for FORMAT via node:net isIP() (not just length) — a malformed IP would otherwise save but never match clientIp(). banUser/banIp serialize per-target (in-memory chain keyed user:<id>/ip:<ip>) so concurrent requests / double-clicks cannot create duplicate active bans (the active-ban uniqueness is temporal so it can't be a static DB unique index; single-instance/single-admin assumption). Mutation + audit are two writes (not one cross-capability tx) — accepted residual, same as Phase 2.

Phase 3.2 chat moderation (ChatModerationService, migration 0012_chat_blocked_words): an admin-editable blocked-word list is hydrated into memory at startup (NO per-chat-message DB read) and LobbyChat.submit replaces matches with **** via a whole-word UNICODE lookaround `(?<![\p{L}\p{N}])word(?![\p{L}\p{N}])` (so Latvian ā/č/ē match, not ASCII \b); words are normalized trim+lowercase+NFC, AND filter() normalizes the input text to NFC before matching so a word blocked in NFC is also caught when typed in the visually-identical decomposed (NFD) form. Known bypasses (zero-width, homoglyphs, inserted punctuation, leetspeak, inflections) are accepted+documented for this minimal first pass. LobbyChat.announce posts an 'Admin'-authored message (no rate-limit, no filter); index.ts wires onAnnounce → chat.announce + gatewayHolder.current.broadcast(CHAT_MESSAGE) — single-VPS (other PG-fanout instances get the broadcast event but not the local history entry). Routes: GET/POST /admin/chat/blocked-words, DELETE /admin/chat/blocked-words/:word, POST /admin/chat/announce (CSRF; CORS Allow-Methods now includes DELETE). Admin UI: /bans (list/revoke/ip-ban) + /moderation (blocked-word chips + announce).

Phase 4A analytics (AdminAnalyticsService) is READ-ONLY aggregates over existing tables (NO new migration): overview (totalUsers/new+active 7d/30d/matches/sumCoinBalances/activeBans), daily activity by UTC epoch-day (created_at/86400000 integer division — identical SQLite/PG; activity(days) returns EXACTLY N calendar days incl. today), segments (new/inactive=no successful login since/suspicious=>=5 failed logins, capped 50), and a read-only leaderboard view + config. Routes are GET guard-only; CSV via Content-Disposition. Labels are honest: 'active' = successful-login active users, 'coins' = materialized coin_balances (rows exist only after first getBalance).

Phase 4B governance (AdminPlayerGovernanceService, D5): exportPlayer builds an explicit ALLOWLIST AdminPlayerExport DTO (NEVER password_hash / token hashes) with COMPLETE no-limit reads (exportUserLedger/LoginHistory/Bans). Hard-delete order (non-transactional, snapshot-first): gather full snapshot → audit player.delete with the snapshot in diff_json (the owner-accepted backup; admin_audit_log is the deliberate exception where pre-delete PII is retained) → StoragePort.anonymizeUserInMatches (scrubSeats in storage/matchAnonymize.ts DELETES userId+clientId from matches.players_json — NOT null, so it stays `string | undefined`; keeps seatIndex/corePlayerId/kind/displayId; idempotent; finds rows via players_json[::text] LIKE '%'||user.id||'%', userId is a randomUUID so no wildcard escaping) → AuthStore.hardDeleteUser (FK CASCADE removes dependents; FIRST anonymizes login_attempts PII — username_tried='[deleted]', ip/user_agent NULL — so no identity survives outside the audit snapshot; the row stays for global aggregates with user_id NULL). A failing audit aborts the delete (user survives). GET /admin/players/:id/export is guarded + per-IP rate-limited (20/15min) + Cache-Control:no-store + audited (player.export). DELETE /admin/players/:id requires CSRF. Admin UI /analytics page + a danger-zone (export + type-the-name delete) on the player page. match_events are NOT touched (core playerId only).
Admin panel is FUNCTIONALLY COMPLETE (Phases 0-4) and D2 deployed; future work should treat it as a live production surface.
